calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Apr-18-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Joan Rodman in Propria Persona.

Ms. Rodman provided a copy of a declaration she filed this morning showing the signature of John Crisafi as Trustee of his Father's trust, stated that interest does not accrue on the amount going to Inheritance Funding, that the amount is a flat $15,700.00, that she will file a copy of the e-mail she received from Inheritance Funding's VP confirming that amount and that hiring outside Counsel without prior Court authorization was an error on her part. Ms. Rodman also stated that the properties are adjacent and had been rented by the same parties for a number of years, that they wanted to sell the properties as a compound, but they needed the tenants to move out, and that the tenants ended up not moving out and the properties were sold. In addition, Ms. Rodman stated that Steve lived in near squalor, that this probate was making order out of chaos, that the issue of the tax returns is new to her, that she is also a CPA, that she has not been paid for it, that she has transferred money into the checking account pending approval to make distribution to the cash beneficiaries and that she will be contacting the other Beneficiaries to determine what form she will be using to make those distributions. The Fourth and Final Report on Status of Administration and Petition for Settlement Thereof, etc. is under submission.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Apr-16-2018 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Mar-07-2018 Continued To Apr-16-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Joan Rodman, petitioner in pro per.

Ms. Rodman stated that an Inventory and Appraisal has been filed, but that additional assets were discovered so a small supplement was sent to the Probate Referee, and she is now awaiting the Probate Referee's response. She said that she expects to receive that response and file an amended Invetory and Appraisal within three weeks. The matter was continued to April 16, 2018 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Craig Blackstone/Shirley Le. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung. (Not reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Feb-07-2018 Continued To Mar-07-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Joan C. Rodman, petitioner in pro per.

Ms. Rodman advised the Court that the Inventory and Appraisal was with the probate referee, and that she would be filing the petition for final distribution when the inventroy and appraisal was completed by the referee. She reported that there had been difficulties removing tenants form one of the two properties owned by the estate, and that there had been challenges in marketing the two properties. She said that both properties have now been sold. The Court expressed concern regarding the delay in filing of the required inventory and appraisal, and directed that an inventory and appraisal be filed within 30 days. He also directed the filing of either a petition for final distributioon of a petition for approval of status of administration within 30 days. The matter was continued to March 7, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung. (Not reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Jan-10-2018 Continued To Feb-07-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Joan Rodman appeared by telephone in Propria Persona.

Ms. Rodman stated that escrow closed on the Sagamore property on December 28th and on the rental property on December 22nd, that initially the Beneficiary wanted to do a buy out with the tenants, that they could not reach an agreement with the tenants, that they then tried to sell the property as a compound and that it did not work. Ms. Rodman also stated that the bills have been paid, that no distribution has been made, that she intends to get the inventory and appraisal filed within 30-days and a petition for final distribution shortly thereafter. The Court expressed concern about attempts to sell the property without an inventory and appraisal, and stated that the inventory was due four months after appointment. Mr. Rodman stated that she filed a notice of proposed action and received no objection. Status on close of estate is continued to February 7, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Paul H. Alvarado. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.
